title: Chlorovirus DNA Miniprep Procedure
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbd2i","text":"MATERIALS:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c2l8o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"45jl7","text":"1) 60-65°C heat block or water bath ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"16mip","text":"2) Microfuge ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d344h","text":"3) 1.5 and 2.0 mL microfuge tubes (screw-cap) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1t6vs","text":"4) 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":37,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"31b3d","text":"5) 10% NP-40 or Triton X-100 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1vbf2","text":"6) DNAse I, 2.0 mg/mL in 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6e8ej","text":"       Store in aliquots at -20°C. Do not refreeze unused material, discard. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8fp0j","text":"7) Proteinase K, 2.0 mg/mL in 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0pde","text":"       Solution can be thawed and frozen several times, but should be discarded after that. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"gleh","text":"8) 10% Na sarcosyl ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ma20","text":"9) CHCl3:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":7,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"flacd","text":"10) 500 mM EDTA, pH 8.0 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3s0t","text":"11) 3 M NaOAc ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4rv27","text":"12) 100% EtOH ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dam2s","text":"13) Tris buffer-saturated phenol ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fd542","text":"14) 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA (1X TE)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Infect appropriate host with desired Chlorovirus species at moi of ~0.001-0.01.
Incubate the samples at 25°C for 24-72 hours, with continuous light and shaking. 72:00:00
Centrifuge 30 mL of the lysates in the Sorvall SS34 rotor at 5,000 rpm (3,000 rcf), 5 min, 4°C. 00:05:00
Add 10% NP-40 (or Triton X-100) to the lysate supernatants for a final concentration of 1%.
Centrifuge the material in a Beckman Ti 50.2 rotor at 15,000 rpm (~27,000 rcf max ), 75 min, 4°C. 01:15:00
Resuspend the virus pellets with 1.0 mL of 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl 2 .
Transfer 500 µL of the resuspended virus to 1.5 ml screw-cap microfuge tubes.
Add 9 µL of DNAse I and mix.
Add 6.0 µL of 500 mM EDTA, pH 8.0 to the samples and mix.
Add 300 µL of buffer-saturated phenol and 300 µL of CHCl 3 :Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) to the tubes.
Centrifuge in the microfuge at maximum speed for 5 min at 4°C. 00:05:00
Remove the upper aqueous layers to clean tubes.
Add 600 µL of CHCl 3 :Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) to the tubes.
Remove the upper aqueous layers to clean tubes and repeat the CHCl 3 :Isoamyl alcohol extraction 1X.
Add 66 µL of 3 M NaOAc to each tube.
Precipitate the DNAs with 2X volumes (approximately 1350 µL) of 100% EtOH.
Centrifuge the tubes in the microfuge for 10-15 min at 4°C to pellet the DNAs. 00:15:00
Wash the DNA pellets 1X with 1000 µL of 70% EtOH in the microfuge for 5 min at 4°C. 00:05:00
Resuspend the DNAs with approximately 60 µL of 1X TE buffer.
Store the DNAs at 4°C.
Add 57 µL of proteinase K and 29 µL of 10% Na sarcosyl and mix.
Dry the pellets briefly (10-15 min) in the vacuum desiccator to remove the EtOH. 00:15:00 Optional: Use speed vac (5 min) instead of vacuum desiccator to remove the EtOH.
Save the supernatants.
Discard the supernatants.
Incubate at room temperature for 60 min. 01:00:00
Incubate the samples at 60-65°C for 60 min. 01:00:00
Mix by inversion.
Centrifuge for 5 min at 4°C in the microfuge. 00:05:00
Mix by inversion.
Place the last extraction into 2.0 mL microfuge tubes.
Mix well and hold at -20°C overnight. 18:00:00
Discard the supernatants.
If the DNA doesn’t go into solution overnight, centrifuge in the microfuge for 15 min at 4°C. 00:15:00
Remove the supernatants to clean tubes.
Discard the pellets.
creator: Irina Agarkova
